medications.indication_rationale: Do not start these over telehealth before an ECG and diagnosis; they are specialist-guided.
An abdominal binder plus sensible salt/fluids reduces splanchnic/lower-limb pooling in hEDS-POTS; advise applying the binder before standing.
contraindications_in_this_case: Confirm no renal/cardiac contraindication to salt loading

medications.indication_rationale: Simple analgesia within limits and joint bracing for transfers support the hypermobility pain and stability.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Hypermobility-specialist physiotherapy for joint stabilisation and graded POTS reconditioning.
Fall-safety, seated ADLs and home aids.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Mainstay non-drug measures for hEDS-POTS pooling.
Protect hypermobile joints and reduce falls during transfers.
No evidence base for POTS/hEDS flares; not recommended.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply the abdominal binder before standing, rise slowly, do all grooming/dressing seated, brace joints for transfers, and watch for black stools/vomiting blood, a locked dislocation, true fainting or chest pain (call 000 if they occur).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces pooling/falls and safety-nets bleeding/dislocation/cardiac emergencies.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Increase fluids and dietary salt sensibly and pre-load fluids before rising.

patient_education_points.point: Your stretchy connective tissue lets blood pool when you're upright, so your heart races and your vision grays out - it settles lying down. That fits POTS from your hEDS, and the joint pain is the hypermobility.
Because a racing heart can occasionally be a rhythm problem, we still want a heart tracing (ECG) and a joint check in person, so please arrange an urgent review.
An abdominal binder, salty fluids before standing, seated activities and joint bracing can help now; we won't start heart or blood-pressure medicines over a chat before you're assessed.
Call 000 if you pass black/tarry stools or vomit blood, a joint pops out and stays deformed/stuck, you actually faint, or you get chest pain.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent in-person GP review within a few days (ECG + joints); dysautonomia/hypermobility follow-up afterwards
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: ECG, postural vitals, joint stability, binder/salt-fluid plan, fall-safety, medication need
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: GI bleeding, a locked dislocation, true syncope or chest pain - call 000.

must_include_items: Screen cardiac (rest palpitations/chest pain/syncope), occult bleeding and dislocation
Advise abdominal binder, salt/fluids, seated ADLs and joint bracing; do NOT start rate-control/vasoactive drugs over chat
Arrange urgent in-person review (ECG + joints) and manage the very high fall risk
Safety-net GI bleeding, locked dislocation, true syncope and chest pain (000)
acceptable_alternatives: Same-day ED if any red-flag feature is present
errors_of_commission: Reflexive ambulance for a settles-supine POTS flare
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) cardiac/bleeding/dislocation screening with an ECG pathway, (2) supportive binder/salt-fluids/bracing without remote drugs, and (3) urgent in-person review plus fall-safety. Ignoring the fall risk or prescribing rate-control drugs on demand is a fail.
